1	package git
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"os/exec"
8		"strings"
9		"sync"
10	)
11	
12	// Command returns a prepared *exec.Cmd for a git invocation that honors
13	// cmd.Dir / -C <path> rather than whatever GIT_DIR / GIT_WORK_TREE / etc.
14	// were set in the parent environment.
15	//
16	// Why this wrapper exists (ddx-aa8a5fb3):
17	// Git's repository-discovery rules mean that if GIT_DIR is set in the
18	// process environment, git uses THAT repo and ignores both -C <path> and
19	// the process's working directory for repository selection. ddx commands
20	// invoked from a git hook (lefthook pre-commit, pre-push, etc.) inherit
21	// the hook's GIT_DIR / GIT_WORK_TREE / GIT_INDEX_FILE. Without stripping
22	// those, any exec.Command("git", ...) inside ddx mutates the hook's
23	// repository (the outer one), producing core.bare=true flips and stray
24	// core.worktree entries in .git/config.
25	//
26	// The wrapper strips exactly git's "local env vars" set (the vars git
27	// itself clears when spawning worktree subprocesses). This is the
28	// canonical set documented by `git rev-parse --local-env-vars`. It
29	// preserves everything else, including GIT_SSH_COMMAND, GIT_TRACE*,
30	// GIT_AUTHOR/COMMITTER_NAME/EMAIL, and custom debugging overrides.
31	//
32	// Dir must be an absolute or working-directory-relative path; it is set
33	// on the returned cmd so that when no GIT_* local vars are in the
34	// environment, git discovers the repo from Dir.
35	func Command(ctx context.Context, dir string, args ...string) *exec.Cmd {
36		warnOnce.Do(maybeWarnGitDirLeaked)
37	
38		cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "git", args...)
39		if dir != "" {
40			cmd.Dir = dir
41		}
42		cmd.Env = CleanEnv()
43		return cmd
44	}
45	
46	// CleanEnv returns os.Environ() with every git local-env-var removed.
47	// Exported so sibling packages (e.g. internal/agent) that spawn NON-git
48	// subprocesses inside a worktree can use the same scrub — those child
49	// processes often invoke git themselves and need the same protection.
50	func CleanEnv() []string {
51		vars := localEnvVars()
52		src := os.Environ()
53		out := make([]string, 0, len(src))
54		for _, kv := range src {
55			eq := strings.IndexByte(kv, '=')
56			if eq < 0 {
57				out = append(out, kv)
58				continue
59			}
60			if vars[kv[:eq]] {
61				continue
62			}
63			out = append(out, kv)
64		}
65		return out
66	}
67	
68	// localEnvVars returns the set of environment variable names that git
69	// treats as repository-local (i.e. scoped to the current git process's
70	// repository selection and cleared when git itself spawns subprocesses
71	// for other worktrees). Result is cached for process lifetime.
72	//
73	// We query `git rev-parse --local-env-vars` once at first use so we pick
74	// up any vars a newer git version adds. If that query fails (git not on
75	// PATH, or restricted environment), fall back to the documented set.
76	func localEnvVars() map[string]bool {
77		localEnvVarsOnce.Do(func() {
78			set := map[string]bool{}
79			// Use exec.Command directly here to avoid recursion through
80			// Command(). This runs with inherited env on purpose: even if
81			// GIT_DIR is set, `git rev-parse --local-env-vars` is a static
82			// list of variable NAMES (not their values), so inherited env
83			// doesn't affect the output.
84			out, err := exec.Command("git", "rev-parse", "--local-env-vars").Output()
85			if err == nil {
86				for _, name := range strings.Fields(string(out)) {
87					if name != "" {
88						set[name] = true
89					}
90				}
91			}
92			// Fallback / supplement: the canonical set from git's source
93			// (environment.c local_repo_env[]). Kept inline so ddx remains
94			// functional even if `git rev-parse` is unavailable.
95			for _, name := range []string{
96				"GIT_DIR",
97				"GIT_WORK_TREE",
98				"GIT_INDEX_FILE",
99				"GIT_COMMON_DIR",
100				"GIT_OBJECT_DIRECTORY",
101				"GIT_ALTERNATE_OBJECT_DIRECTORIES",
102				"GIT_PREFIX",
103				"GIT_INTERNAL_GETTEXT_SH_SCHEME",
104				"GIT_SHALLOW_FILE",
105				"GIT_NO_REPLACE_OBJECTS",
106				"GIT_REPLACE_REF_BASE",
107				"GIT_CONFIG",
108				"GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL",
109				"GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM",
110				"GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS",
111				"GIT_CONFIG_COUNT",
112				"GIT_INDEX_VERSION",
113				"GIT_LITERAL_PATHSPECS",
114				"GIT_GLOB_PATHSPECS",
115				"GIT_NOGLOB_PATHSPECS",
116				"GIT_ICASE_PATHSPECS",
117				"GIT_QUARANTINE_PATH",
118				"GIT_INTERNAL_NEW_OBJECT_HOOK",
119				"GIT_IMPLICIT_WORK_TREE",
120			} {
121				set[name] = true
122			}
123			localEnvVarsCache = set
124		})
125		return localEnvVarsCache
126	}
127	
128	var (
129		localEnvVarsOnce  sync.Once
130		localEnvVarsCache map[string]bool
131	
132		warnOnce sync.Once
133	)

135	// maybeWarnGitDirLeaked emits a one-time stderr diagnostic when the
136	// parent environment has GIT_DIR set. This is the canonical smoke signal
137	// for hook-inherited env contamination; surfacing it during normal runs
138	// helps catch upstream leaks we haven't yet wrapped.
139	func maybeWarnGitDirLeaked() {
140		if v := os.Getenv("GIT_DIR"); v != "" {
141			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr,
142				"ddx git wrapper: stripped inherited GIT_DIR=%q (likely hook context). "+
143					"This is diagnostic; operation proceeds against cmd.Dir instead.\n", v)
144		}
145	}

44	const beadReviewInstructions = `You are reviewing a bead implementation against its acceptance criteria.
45	
46	## Your task
47	
48	Examine the diff and each acceptance-criteria (AC) item. For each item assign one grade:
49	
50	- **APPROVE** — fully and correctly implemented; cite the specific file path and line that proves it.
51	- **REQUEST_CHANGES** — partially implemented or has fixable minor issues.
52	- **BLOCK** — not implemented, incorrectly implemented, or the diff is insufficient to evaluate.
53	
54	Overall verdict rule:
55	- All items APPROVE → **APPROVE**
56	- Any item BLOCK → **BLOCK**
57	- Otherwise → **REQUEST_CHANGES**
58	
59	## Required output format
60	
61	Respond with a structured review using exactly this layout (replace placeholder text):
62	
63	---
64	## Review: <bead-id> iter <N>
65	
66	### Verdict: APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES | BLOCK
67	
68	### AC Grades
69	
70	| # | Item | Grade | Evidence |
71	|---|------|-------|----------|
72	| 1 | <AC item text, max 60 chars> | APPROVE | path/to/file.go:42 — brief note |
73	| 2 | <AC item text, max 60 chars> | BLOCK   | — not found in diff |
74	
75	### Summary
76	
77	<1–3 sentences on overall implementation quality and any recurring theme in findings.>
78	
79	### Findings
80	
81	<Bullet list of REQUEST_CHANGES and BLOCK findings. Each finding must name the specific file, function, or test that is missing or wrong — specific enough for the next agent to act on without re-reading the entire diff. Omit this section entirely if verdict is APPROVE.>`

83	func (f *CommandFactory) newBeadReviewCommand() *cobra.Command {
84		cmd := &cobra.Command{
85			Use:   "review <id>",
86			Short: "Generate a review prompt for a bead's implementation",
87			Long: `Generate a review-ready prompt for a bead implementation.
88	
89	The prompt includes:
90	  - Bead title, description, and acceptance criteria
91	  - Full content of governing documents (spec-id refs from the bead)
92	  - Git diff of the reviewed commit (git show)
93	  - Review instructions with the expected APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK output contract
94	
95	By default the commit is taken from the bead's closing_commit_sha field.
96	Use --from-rev to override.
97	
98	Pipe the output into ddx agent run:
99	
100	  ddx bead review <id> | ddx agent run --prompt @-
101	  ddx bead review <id> --output /tmp/review.md && ddx agent run --prompt /tmp/review.md`,
102			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
103			RunE: f.runBeadReview,
104		}
105		cmd.Flags().String("from-rev", "", "Commit SHA to review (default: closing_commit_sha from bead)")
106		cmd.Flags().Int("iter", 1, "Review iteration number (shown in prompt header and grade table)")
107		cmd.Flags().String("output", "", "Write prompt to file instead of stdout")
108		return cmd
109	}
110	
111	func (f *CommandFactory) runBeadReview(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
112		beadID := args[0]
113		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from-rev")
114		iter, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("iter")
115		outputFile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("output")
116	
117		s := f.beadStore()
118		b, err := s.Get(beadID)
119		if err != nil {
120			return err
121		}
122	
123		// Resolve the commit SHA to review.
124		// Priority: --from-rev flag > closing_commit_sha on the bead.
125		rev := strings.TrimSpace(fromRev)
126		if rev == "" {
127			if sha, ok := b.Extra["closing_commit_sha"].(string); ok {
128				rev = strings.TrimSpace(sha)
129			}
130		}
131		if rev == "" {
132			return fmt.Errorf("no commit to review: use --from-rev <sha>, or close the bead with a commit SHA first (ddx bead close --commit <sha>)")
133		}
134	
135		// Project root is used for git operations and governing doc file reads.
136		projectRoot := gitpkg.FindProjectRoot(f.WorkingDir)
137		if projectRoot == "" {
138			projectRoot = f.WorkingDir
139		}
140	
141		// Fetch the git diff for the commit.
142		diff, err := beadReviewGitShow(projectRoot, rev)
143		if err != nil {
144			return fmt.Errorf("git show %s: %w", rev, err)
145		}
146	
147		// Resolve governing document references from the bead's spec-id field.
148		refs := agent.ResolveGoverningRefs(projectRoot, b)
149	
150		// Build and emit the review prompt.
151		var sb strings.Builder
152		buildBeadReviewPrompt(&sb, b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs)
153	
154		prompt := sb.String()
155		if outputFile != "" {
156			return os.WriteFile(outputFile, []byte(prompt), 0o644)
157		}
158		_, err = fmt.Fprint(cmd.OutOrStdout(), prompt)
159		return err
160	}
161	
162	// beadReviewGitShow runs `git show <rev>` with pathspec exclusions for
163	// execution-evidence noise so the review prompt's <diff> section stays
164	// bounded. See agent.EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs and ddx-39e27896.
165	func beadReviewGitShow(projectRoot, rev string) (string, error) {
166		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
167		defer cancel()
168		args := append([]string{"-C", projectRoot, "show", rev, "--", "."}, agent.EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs()...)
169		out, err := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "git", args...).Output()
170		if err != nil {
171			return "", err
172		}
173		return string(out), nil
174	}
175	
176	// buildBeadReviewPrompt writes the complete review prompt to sb.
177	func buildBeadReviewPrompt(sb *strings.Builder, b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []agent.GoverningRef) {
178		sb.WriteString("<bead-review>\n")
179	
180		// ── Bead section ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
181		fmt.Fprintf(sb, "  <bead id=%q iter=%d>\n", b.ID, iter)
182		fmt.Fprintf(sb, "    <title>%s</title>\n", beadReviewXMLEscape(strings.TrimSpace(b.Title)))
183	
184		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
185			fmt.Fprintf(sb, "    <description>\n%s\n    </description>\n", beadReviewXMLEscape(desc))
186		} else {
187			sb.WriteString("    <description/>\n")
188		}
189	
190		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
191			fmt.Fprintf(sb, "    <acceptance>\n%s\n    </acceptance>\n", beadReviewXMLEscape(acc))
192		} else {
193			sb.WriteString("    <acceptance/>\n")
194		}
195	
196		if len(b.Labels) > 0 {
197			fmt.Fprintf(sb, "    <labels>%s</labels>\n", beadReviewXMLEscape(strings.Join(b.Labels, ", ")))
198		}
199	
200		sb.WriteString("  </bead>\n\n")
